pi_items.source: Task description specifies this as the column name to add. Natural Earth dataset includes 'pop_est' (population estimate) attribute which will be used in the density calculation.
Verified in setup_geoserver.sh (line 185) and setup_task.sh (line 51). Layer is published as 'ne_countries' in workspace 'ne'.
Verified in task.json description and export_result.sh (line 82). Output directory is created in setup_task.sh (line 10).
World population density research: minimum density countries include Greenland (~0.1 people/km²) and other sparse regions. Zero is a reasonable lower bound.
World population density research: Monaco has the highest density at ~25,927 people/km² (2025). 50,000 provides a safe upper bound exceeding all real-world countries.

Natural Earth documentation and GitHub repository confirm that the ne_110m_admin_0_countries shapefile contains 177 features. While Natural Earth conceptually covers 258 countries, the 110m scale dataset is simplified to 177 features, with smaller countries omitted. This is documented in rnaturalearthdata R package and GitHub issue discussions.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the Natural Earth 1:110m Admin 0 Countries dataset, which is real public domain geospatial data. The dataset contains exactly 177 features. The task adds a 'pop_density' column calculated from 'pop_est' (population estimate) divided by area in square kilometers. Population density thresholds are realistic: minimum of 0 people/km² (matching the sparsest countries like Greenland) and maximum of 50,000 people/km² (exceeding Monaco's ~25,927 people/km², the world's most densely populated country). The layer 'ne:ne_countries' is published in GeoServer workspace 'ne', and the output is saved to '/home/ga/output/wfs_countries_with_density.json'.
